name: Spinach Timbales
recipeIngredient: 2 large Eggs, at room temperature, separated
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Spray four 6-ounce custard cups with nonstick cooking spray.
3. Dust the cups evenly with 1/2 teaspoon flour; set aside.
4. In a medium nonstick saucepan over medium heat, melt butter substitute.
5. Add onion and sauté for 5 minutes until tender.
6. Whisk in remaining flour; cook, stirring milk continuously about 3 minutes until thickened.
7. In a small bowl, beat egg yolks.
8. Stir in 2 tablespoons of the hot milk mixture into the egg yolks.
9. Return all to the saucepan and cook for 30 seconds.
10. Remove from heat; stir in spinach and nutmeg.
11. Scrape into a large bowl and cover.
12. In a small bowl, with an electric mixer on high speed, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry.
13. Stir 1/4 of the whites into the spinach mixture to lighten it; fold in the remaining whites until no white streaks remain.
14. Spoon evenly into cups.
15. Smooth tops and place cups on a baking sheet.
16. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until browned. Makes 4 servings, 123 calories per serving.
